name: Jinx
playstyleSummary: Jinx is a high-damage marksman who swaps between a rapid-fire minigun for close fights and a slower rocket launcher for poke and waveclear. She feels explosive in the late game - once she's fed and ramped up, few champions can out-damage her in a straight fight.
strengths: Massive scaling damage output in the late game
Super Mega Death Rocket threatens kills across the entire map
Flame Chompers give strong zone control and root potential
Passive grants huge speed after securing objectives or kills, letting her snowball
weaknesses: Very weak and immobile in the early game
No dash or blink, making her easy to catch when isolated
Minigun needs time to ramp up attack speed before it's effective
Flame Chompers have a long cooldown, leaving her without an escape if she misses

abilities.name: Get Excited!
abilities.howToUseIt: After you help kill an enemy champion, epic monster, or structure, you get a big burst of move and attack speed - chain kills and objectives together to snowball a lead.

abilities.name: Switcheroo!
abilities.howToUseIt: Switch to Pow-Pow (minigun) for attack speed in close fights, or Fishbones (rocket launcher) for longer range and area damage at the cost of mana and attack speed - pick based on whether you want to poke or duel.

abilities.name: Zap!
abilities.howToUseIt: Fire this at a fleeing or approaching enemy to slow and reveal them - it's your main tool for finishing a kill or spotting someone diving you.

abilities.name: Flame Chompers!
abilities.howToUseIt: Throw a line of these before a fight or across a chokepoint; they root any enemy champion who walks over them, so use them defensively to stop a diver or offensively to lock down a target.

abilities.name: Super Mega Death Rocket!
abilities.howToUseIt: Fire this across the map at a low-health enemy - it deals more damage the lower their health is, so save it to finish kills rather than to open a fight.

earlyGamePlan: Play safely in lane since Jinx is weak before she has items - use Fishbones' rockets to poke and clear waves from a distance rather than trading in melee. Farm patiently and wait for teamfights and objectives, where her passive speed boost and scaling damage start to take over games.
quickTips: Switch to Fishbones for poke and waveclear, and to Pow-Pow when a fight gets close.
Save Flame Chompers to stop a diver or root a target - don't throw them randomly, since the cooldown is long.
Use your ultimate to finish low-health targets, not to start a fight.
Stay near allies early since you have no dash and are easy to catch.
Chain kills and objectives together to keep your passive speed boost active and snowball.
Position at the edge of teamfights and let your minigun ramp up once it's safe.
whatToExpect: An enemy Jinx is dangerous once she gets going, so try to shut her down early before she has items - she has no dash, so a well-timed gank can punish her hard. Watch for her rockets poking from a distance, and be very careful sitting at low health anywhere on the map, since her ultimate can execute you from across the screen.

glossary.definition: Turning an early advantage into an even bigger one, making a strong player or team harder to stop over time.
How much stronger a champion gets over time as they gain levels and items, relative to others.
The ability to quickly kill a wave of minions, useful for controlling lane and pushing.
Chipping away at enemy health from range without committing to a full fight.
A short, fast movement ability that repositions a champion, often used to escape or engage.
When a jungler or ally ambushes an enemy laner to help get a kill or lead.
A fight involving most or all champions from both teams at once.
